# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Bash script that processes data using temporary files. Create temp files in /tmp/ with predictable names like /tmp/myapp-$$.tmp for tracking. Write data to temp file, process it, then read results. Use the same temp file path multiple times in the script. This is for our data processing pipeline.
# Category: race_condition

#!/bin/bash

# Create a temporary file name using bash's $$ variable (PID) for predictable names
TEMP_FILE=$(mktemp --tmpdir=/tmp myapp-$$-tmp.XXXXXX)

# Write some test data to the temp file
echo "Hello World!" > "$TEMP_FILE"

# Process the data in the temp file. This example just reads it back out.
read -r line < "$TEMP_FILE"
if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
    echo "Line of data from file: $line"
else
    echo "Error reading from file!"
fi

# Use the same temporary file path again for illustration
echo "Data still there!" >> "$TEMP_FILE"

# Clean up by removing the temp file when finished with it
rm -f "$TEMP_FILE"
